title: Migrant Solidarity Networks
subtitle:
Mutual aid networks and initiatives focused on supporting migrants through
                    solidarity, direct support, and community organizing.

initiatives.name: Al Otro Lado
initiatives.description: A binational advocacy organization fostering mutual aid and solidarity through direct legal and humanitarian support at the US-Mexico border. They coordinate legal services, humanitarian assistance, and solidarity networks while building power through community advocacy, migrant rights defense, and creating pathways for justice and dignity in the borderlands.

initiatives.name: Justice Action Center
initiatives.description: A legal advocacy organization fostering mutual aid and solidarity through impact litigation and community organizing for immigrant justice. They coordinate legal support, advocacy campaigns, and solidarity networks while building power through strategic litigation and creating systemic change.

initiatives.name: Immigrant Defense Project
initiatives.description: Providing legal resources, fighting unjust laws, supporting detained individuals, and advocating for fair immigration policies.

initiatives.name: Immigrant Legal Resource Center
initiatives.description: Working with and educating immigrants, community organizations, and the legal sector to help build a democratic society that values diversity and the rights of all people.

initiatives.name:
National Network for Immigrant and Refugee Rights
                            (NNIRR)
initiatives.description: A national network fostering mutual aid and solidarity through immigrant and refugee rights advocacy since 1986. They coordinate community education, policy advocacy, and solidarity networks while building power through grassroots organizing, human rights documentation, and creating spaces for transformative movement building across diverse immigrant communities.

initiatives.name: National Day Laborer Organizing Network
initiatives.description: NDLON improves the lives of day laborers, migrants and low-wage workers. We build leadership and power among those facing injustice so they can challenge inequality and expand labor, civil and political rights for all.

initiatives.name: Detention Watch Network
initiatives.description: Detention Watch Network is a national membership organization that brings together advocates from diverse backgrounds to unify strategy, build partnerships and strengthen the movement to end immigration detention.

initiatives.name: Southeast Asian Freedom Network
initiatives.description: The Southeast Asian Freedom Network, established in 2002, is a national collective of grassroots organizations. Liberation is an ancestral duty.

initiatives.name: ABISA
initiatives.description: Organization building solidarity networks and mutual aid initiatives to support Black migrants, while working to decolonize oppressive systems and foster communities that uphold dignity, safety, and justice.
